tactic_definitions.Evidence sharing: providing concrete facts to support a claim
tactic_definitions.Perspective-taking: acknowledging another's viewpoint to build trust
tactic_definitions.Defensive disclosure: revealing only what is necessary to protect oneself
tactic_definitions.Self-justification: excusing one's own actions to avoid blame
tactic_definitions.Weaponized candor: using blunt truth strategically to gain advantage
tactic_definitions.Truthful misdirection: giving accurate details that nonetheless distract
tactic_definitions.Misleading emphasis: highlighting true but minor points to distort perception
tactic_definitions.Bayesian hedging: presenting probabilities or uncertainty to remain flexible
tactic_definitions.Pragmatic silence: omitting information instead of lying outright
tactic_definitions.Misframing: presenting information in a skewed context
tactic_definitions.Withholding info (WIFOM): creating doubt by hiding critical details
tactic_definitions.Strategic omission: leaving out facts to influence interpretation
tactic_definitions.Noisy sincerity: being honest in a confusing or overloaded way
tactic_definitions.Over-explaining: adding excessive detail to appear genuine
tactic_definitions.Face-saving blather: using empty talk to avoid confrontation
tactic_definitions.Vague filler: relying on imprecise or generic phrases
tactic_definitions.Bullshitting: speaking without concern for truth or falsity
tactic_definitions.Technobabble: using jargon to appear credible without substance
tactic_definitions.Honest mistake: presenting something false in good faith
tactic_definitions.Deflection: shifting blame or suspicion away from oneself by redirecting attention to another player or event, often using partial truths or emotional appeals
tactic_definitions.Rationalized error: justifying a mistake with faulty logic
tactic_definitions.Reverse accusations: shifting blame back onto the accuser
tactic_definitions.Strategic fabrication: inventing details to achieve a goal
tactic_definitions.Hard lying: deliberately asserting the opposite of the truth
tactic_definitions.Gaslighting: making others doubt their memory or perception

levels.Moderate: Generally rational with some uncertainty or hedging
levels.Low: Less structured reasoning, may be confused or overwhelmed

levels.High: Highly skilled manipulator, subtle deception, strategic planning
levels.Moderate: Competent deceiver, uses standard tactics, some slips
levels.Low: Clumsy deceiver, obvious tells, defensive, may contradict self
